info.name: NY Vehicle Service Tier Flow
info.description:
End-to-end tests for vehicle service tier management and all usageRestriction
checks in selectVehicleTierForDriverWithUsageRestriction.

Drivers onboarded:
  A   — fresh SEDAN, no special restrictions (baseline + main test driver)
  B   — SEDAN with mYManufacturing=2005-01-01 (~20 years, tests mfcCheck)
  C   — SEDAN with airConditioned=false (tests airConditionedCheck at onboarding)
  AUTO— AUTO_RICKSHAW (tests variant-based filterVehicleTier)

Test phases:
  2. Structure & canSwitchToAirport field validation (new field)
  3. Ineligibility: AUTO driver has no CAB tiers (filterVehicleTier)
  4a. mfcCheck: old vehicle (2005) restricts vehicleAgeThreshold-gated tiers
  4b. airConditionedCheck: AC=false at onboarding restricts AC-gated tiers
  5. Tier selection: driver can select non-restricted tiers;
     defaultForVehicleVariant tier is always kept selected
  6. vehicleRatingCheck: dashboard updateVehicleRating(1.0)→restricted, (5.0)→unrestricted
  7. airConditionedCheck (dashboard path): acRestriction/update(isWorking=false)
     restricts AC-gated tiers AND auto-deselects them (shouldUpdateServiceTiers=true)
  8. airConditionedCheck (driver path): updateAirCondition(false) same behaviour
  9. canSwitchToAirport toggle via updateServiceTiers

Not covered (no API to set values directly):
  luggageCapacityCheck — luggageCapacity not exposed in addVehicle or any dashboard API
  driverRatingCheck    — DriverStats.rating computed from actual rides only

Prerequisites: local stack running, SEDAN + AUTO service tiers configured for the city.

exec: var rnd = function () { return '9' + Math.floor(100000000 + Math.random() * 899999999); };

item.name: Structure & canSwitchToAirport field (Driver)

exec: // Driver A was onboarded with a SEDAN — must have at least one eligible tier
// canSwitchToAirport is null when no tier has isAirportRideEnabled=true,
// or a boolean (driverInfo.canSwitchToAirport) when at least one non-restricted tier does.
// New field added in the service_tier changes commit — verify it exists.
pm.test('canSwitchToAirport field present in response (new field)', function () {
// isUsageRestricted — set by mapUsageRestriction; tells UI which quality thresholds failed.
// isSelected        — true if this tier is in vehicle.selectedServiceTiers.
// isDefault         — true if vehicle.variant is in tier.defaultForVehicleVariant.
pm.test('each tier has isUsageRestricted / isSelected / isDefault', function () {
// Save for diff-based assertions in later steps
console.log('canSwitchToAirport:', d.canSwitchToAirport, '| canSwitchToRental:', d.canSwitchToRental, '| canSwitchToInterCity:', d.canSwitchToInterCity);

item.description:
GET /driver/vehicleServiceTiers — response shape validation.

Checks:
  • tiers[] non-empty (driver has at least one eligible tier)
  • canSwitchToAirport field present — new field from service_tier commit;
    computed as: if any non-restricted tier has isAirportRideEnabled
    then Just driverInfo.canSwitchToAirport else Nothing
  • Every tier has isUsageRestricted / isSelected / isDefault

Saves full response as _vst_baseline for diff-based assertions in later steps.

item.name: Variant filter — AUTO has no CAB tiers (Driver)

exec: // AUTO_RICKSHAW is NOT in allowedVehicleVariant of CAB-category tiers,
// so SEDAN/SUV/HATCHBACK/TAXI must not appear in the response at all.
// This is different from isUsageRestricted=true: the tier doesn't exist for this driver.
pm.test('AUTO driver: no CAB tiers in response (filterVehicleTier works)', function () {
'Unexpected CAB tiers for AUTO_RICKSHAW driver: ' + cabFound.map(function(t){return t.serviceTierType;}).join(','));

item.description:
GET /driver/vehicleServiceTiers for Driver AUTO (AUTO_RICKSHAW).

Tests filterVehicleTier in selectVehicleTierForDriverWithUsageRestriction:
  filterVehicleTier tier = vehicle.variant `elem` tier.allowedVehicleVariant

AUTO_RICKSHAW is not in allowedVehicleVariant of any CAB tier, so
SEDAN / SUV / HATCHBACK / TAXI must be completely absent — not just restricted.
This is ineligibility: the tier is filtered out, not marked with isUsageRestricted.

item.name: mfcCheck — old vehicle (2005) restricts age-gated tiers (Driver)

exec: //   mfcCheck = case (Just (Months age), Just (Months threshold)) -> age < threshold
// We detect these by diffing against Driver A (no mYManufacturing → mfcCheck always True).
var at = baseA.find(function(t){ return t.serviceTierType === bt.serviceTierType; });
return at && !at.isUsageRestricted; // restricted for B but not A → mfcCheck caused it
pm.test('mfcCheck: 2005 vehicle restricts vehicleAgeThreshold-gated tiers (fresh vehicle not restricted)', function () {
console.log('[info] No vehicleAgeThreshold-gated tiers in city config — mfcCheck N/A');
if (ageRestr.length > 0) console.log('Age-restricted (B not A):', ageRestr.map(function(t){return t.serviceTierType;}).join(','));

item.description:
GET /driver/vehicleServiceTiers for Driver B (mYManufacturing=2005-01-01).

Tests mfcCheck in selectVehicleTierForDriverWithUsageRestriction:
  vehicleAgeInMonths = getVehicleAge vehicle.mYManufacturing now
  mfcCheck = case (vehicleAgeInMonths, tier.vehicleAgeThreshold) of
    (Just (Months age), Just (Months threshold)) -> age < threshold
    _ -> True

2005-01-01 → age ≈ 240 months. Any tier with vehicleAgeThreshold < 240
has mfcCheck=False → isUsageRestricted=True.

Compares against Driver A baseline (no mYManufacturing → age=Nothing → mfcCheck=True always).
Conditional: passes gracefully if no tier has vehicleAgeThreshold configured.

item.name: airConditionedCheck — AC off restricts AC tiers (Driver)

exec: // Driver C: vehicle.airConditioned = Just False → condition fails for any AC-gated tier.
// We identify AC-gated tiers by the airConditioned field in the response (non-null = has threshold).
var acGated = d.tiers.filter(function(t){ return t.airConditioned !== null && t.airConditioned !== undefined; });
pm.test('airConditionedCheck: vehicle.airConditioned=false restricts all AC-gated tiers', function () {
'Expected restricted (vehicle.airConditioned=Just False), but found unrestricted: ' + notRestr.map(function(t){return t.serviceTierType;}).join(','));
// Cross-check: same tiers must be unrestricted for Driver A.
// Driver A: airConditioned not passed in addVehicle → vehicle.airConditioned=Nothing.
// isNothing Nothing = True → short-circuits the check → not restricted.
var at = baseA.find(function(t){ return t.serviceTierType === ct.serviceTierType; });
pm.test('Same AC-gated tiers are unrestricted for Driver A (airConditioned=Nothing)', function () {
'Expected Driver A unrestricted for: ' + acGated.map(function(t){return t.serviceTierType;}).join(','));
console.log('[info] No AC-gated tiers in city config — airConditionedCheck N/A');

item.description:
GET /driver/vehicleServiceTiers for Driver C (addVehicle with airConditioned=false).

Tests airConditionedCheck in selectVehicleTierForDriverWithUsageRestriction:
  airConditionedCheck =
    (tier.vehicleCategory == Just AMBULANCE)
    || (compareNumber tier.airConditionedThreshold driverInfo.airConditionScore
        && (isNothing tier.airConditionedThreshold || vehicle.airConditioned /= Just False))

vehicle.airConditioned = Just False → the second condition fails for any tier
with an airConditionedThreshold → isUsageRestricted=True.

Also cross-checks: same tiers are unrestricted for Driver A (airConditioned=Nothing
→ isNothing Nothing = True → check short-circuits to True → not restricted).

item.name: Select one non-restricted tier (Driver)

exec: // Build request body: select exactly one non-restricted tier, deselect all others.
// Backend honours the isSelected field from the request body when a matching tier exists:
if (!target) { console.log('[skip] no non-restricted tier found'); pm.execution.skipRequest(); return; }
var tiers = base.tiers.map(function(t){ return Object.assign({},t,{isSelected: t.serviceTierType===target}); });

item.description:
POST /driver/updateServiceTiers — driver explicitly selects a tier.

Prerequest builds the body from _vst_baseline, setting isSelected=true
for one non-restricted tier.

Backend in postDriverUpdateServiceTiers reads:
  let isSelected = maybe isAlreadySelected (.isSelected) mbServiceTierDriverRequest
  if isSelected || (isDefault && vehicle.category /= AMBULANCE)
    then return (Just tier)

item.name: Verify selection persisted (Driver)

exec: // QVehicle.updateSelectedServiceTiers persists the new list — verify it worked.
pm.test('Tier selection persisted: isSelected=true for ' + sel, function(){

item.description:
Verifies the tier selection from the previous POST persisted.

QVehicle.updateSelectedServiceTiers is called at the end of postDriverUpdateServiceTiers,
so the next GET must reflect isSelected=true for the tier we sent.

item.name: Deselect all — default tier protected (Driver)

exec: // Backend logic: if isSelected || (isDefault && vehicle.category /= AMBULANCE)
//   then return (Just tier)   -- kept in selectedServiceTiers
// Default tiers (vehicle.variant in tier.defaultForVehicleVariant) survive this.
console.log('Sending all isSelected=false. Default expected to survive:', pm.environment.get('_vst_default_type'));

item.description:
POST /driver/updateServiceTiers with all tiers as isSelected=false.

Tests default-tier protection in postDriverUpdateServiceTiers:
  if isSelected || (isDefault && vehicle.category /= AMBULANCE)
    then return (Just tier)   -- kept

Where isDefault = vehicle.variant `elem` tier.defaultForVehicleVariant.
Backend ignores isSelected=false for default tiers — they stay selected.

item.name: Verify default tier stays selected (Driver)

exec: // must remain isSelected=true even though we sent isSelected=false.
pm.test('defaultForVehicleVariant tier stays selected despite deselect-all request', function(){
console.log('Default tier', dflt, 'isSelected:', t ? t.isSelected : 'NOT FOUND');
} else { console.log('[info] No default tier found in baseline — skipped'); }

item.description:
Verifies the default-tier protection from the previous step.

The tier where vehicle.variant ∈ tier.defaultForVehicleVariant must still
have isSelected=true, because the backend's isDefault check overrides isSelected=false.

item.name: Set vehicle rating 1.0 (Dashboard)
item.description:
Dashboard POST /driverVehicleQuality/updateVehicleRating.

Sets vehicle.vehicleRating=1.0 via updateVehicleRatingAndRemark,
writing to both the vehicle table and the RC.
Used to trigger vehicleRatingCheck restriction in the next GET.

item.name: vehicleRatingCheck — low rating restricts tiers (Driver)

exec: // Detect by diffing against baseline (before we set the low rating).
return b && !b.isUsageRestricted; // was unrestricted, now restricted after rating=1.0
pm.test('vehicleRatingCheck: vehicleRating=1.0 restricts tiers with higher threshold', function(){
console.log('Newly restricted after rating=1.0:', newlyRestr.map(function(t){return t.serviceTierType;}).join(','));
console.log('[info] No vehicleRating-gated tiers in city config, or threshold ≤ 1.0');

item.description:
Tests vehicleRatingCheck in selectVehicleTierForDriverWithUsageRestriction:
  vehicleRatingCheck = compareNumber vehicle.vehicleRating tier.vehicleRating
  compareNumber (Just 1.0) (Just threshold) = 1.0 >= threshold

Any tier with vehicleRating threshold > 1.0 → vehicleRatingCheck=False → restricted.
Detected by diffing against _vst_baseline_tiers.
Conditional: passes gracefully if no tier has a vehicleRating threshold.

item.name: Restore vehicle rating 5.0 (Dashboard)
item.description: Restores vehicle.vehicleRating=5.0. compareNumber (Just 5.0) (Just threshold) = 5.0 >= threshold passes for all realistic thresholds.

item.name: vehicleRatingCheck — high rating restores tiers (Driver)

exec: // compareNumber (Just 5.0) (Just threshold) = 5.0 >= threshold → True for any reasonable threshold.
pm.test('vehicleRatingCheck: rating=5.0 restores all previously restricted tiers', function(){
} else { console.log('[info] No vehicleRating-gated tiers were found — N/A'); }
// Refresh baseline for the AC phases below

item.description:
Verifies vehicleRatingCheck recovery:
  compareNumber (Just 5.0) (Just threshold) = 5.0 >= threshold → True
→ vehicleRatingCheck=True → tiers that were restricted with 1.0 are now unrestricted.

item.name: AC restriction OFF (Dashboard)
item.description:
Dashboard POST /driver/{id}/acRestriction/update {isWorking:false}.

Calls checkAndUpdateAirConditioned(isDashboard=True, isAirConditioned=False,
  shouldUpdateServiceTiers=True).

Two effects:
  1. vehicle.airConditioned = Just False
  2. incrementDriverAcUsageRestrictionCount → increases driverInfo.airConditionScore

Both contribute to airConditionedCheck failing for AC-gated tiers.

item.name: AC tiers restricted & auto-deselected (Driver)

exec: //   → (isNothing threshold || vehicle.airConditioned /= Just False) evaluates to False
var acGated = d.tiers.filter(function(t){ return t.airConditioned !== null && t.airConditioned !== undefined; });
pm.test('airConditionedCheck: dashboard acRestriction(isWorking=false) restricts all AC-gated tiers', function(){
// So AC-restricted tiers must NOT appear as isSelected=true.
pm.test('Auto-deselect (shouldUpdateServiceTiers=true): AC-restricted tiers removed from selectedServiceTiers', function(){
} else { console.log('[info] No AC-gated tiers in city config — N/A'); }

item.description:
Verifies both effects of dashboard acRestriction/update(isWorking=false):

1. airConditionedCheck fails → isUsageRestricted=true:
     vehicle.airConditioned=Just False → (vehicle.airConditioned /= Just False) = False

2. shouldUpdateServiceTiers=True → auto-deselect:
     fetchVehicleTierForDriverWithUsageRestriction(onlyAutoSelected=True)
     uses autoSelectedVehicleVariant (not allowedVehicleVariant)
     restricted tiers excluded → QVehicle.updateSelectedServiceTiers called
     → AC-restricted tiers must not be isSelected=true

item.name: AC restriction ON — restore (Dashboard)
item.description:
Dashboard POST /driver/{id}/acRestriction/update {isWorking:true}.

Sets vehicle.airConditioned=Just True. airConditionScore is NOT reset — only
incremented by isWorking=false. For a fresh driver, one increment is still below
threshold so the score-based part of airConditionedCheck still passes.

item.name: AC tiers unrestricted after restore (Driver)

exec: // airConditionedCheck: (isNothing threshold || Just True /= Just False) = True → passes.
// airConditionScore was incremented, but one increment for a fresh driver is still below threshold.
var acGated = d.tiers.filter(function(t){ return t.airConditioned !== null && t.airConditioned !== undefined; });
pm.test('AC-gated tiers unrestricted after dashboard acRestriction(isWorking=true)', function(){
} else { console.log('[info] No AC-gated tiers — N/A'); }

item.description:
Verifies recovery after dashboard acRestriction/update(isWorking=true).

vehicle.airConditioned=Just True → (isNothing threshold || Just True /= Just False) = True
→ airConditionedCheck passes → isUsageRestricted=False.

item.name: updateAirCondition OFF (Driver)
item.description:
Driver-facing POST /driver/updateAirCondition {isAirConditioned:false}.

Calls checkAndUpdateAirConditioned(isDashboard=False, isAirConditioned=False,
  shouldUpdateServiceTiers=True).

Difference from dashboard path:
  • Sets vehicle.airConditioned=Just False (same)
  • Does NOT call incrementDriverAcUsageRestrictionCount (airConditionScore unchanged)
  • shouldUpdateServiceTiers=True still fires the auto-deselect

item.name: AC tiers restricted & auto-deselected (Driver)

exec: // Same airConditionedCheck failure as dashboard path:
// Key difference: airConditionScore is NOT incremented (no incrementDriverAcUsageRestrictionCount).
// But shouldUpdateServiceTiers=True still triggers the auto-deselect.
var acGated = d.tiers.filter(function(t){ return t.airConditioned !== null && t.airConditioned !== undefined; });
pm.test('airConditionedCheck: driver updateAirCondition(false) restricts AC-gated tiers', function(){
//   → restricted tiers excluded from new selectedServiceTiers list
pm.test('Auto-update: AC-restricted tiers removed from selectedServiceTiers (driver-side)', function(){
} else { console.log('[info] No AC-gated tiers in city config — N/A'); }

item.description:
Verifies driver-side updateAirCondition(false) path:

1. Same airConditionedCheck failure: vehicle.airConditioned=Just False → restricted.

2. shouldUpdateServiceTiers=True auto-deselect:
     fetchVehicleTierForDriverWithUsageRestriction(onlyAutoSelected=True)
     → uses autoSelectedVehicleVariant
     → restricted tiers excluded from new selectedServiceTiers

item.name: updateAirCondition ON — restore (Driver)
item.description: Restores via driver-side updateAirCondition(true). Sets vehicle.airConditioned=Just True without touching airConditionScore.

item.name: AC tiers unrestricted after restore (Driver)

exec: // vehicle.airConditioned=Just True → (isNothing threshold || Just True /= Just False) = True → check passes.
var acGated = d.tiers.filter(function(t){ return t.airConditioned !== null && t.airConditioned !== undefined; });
pm.test('AC-gated tiers unrestricted after driver updateAirCondition(true)', function(){
} else { console.log('[info] No AC-gated tiers — N/A'); }

item.description: Same recovery as dashboard path: vehicle.airConditioned=Just True → airConditionedCheck passes → tiers unrestricted.

item.name: Toggle canSwitchToAirport (Driver)

exec: // canSwitchToAirport is null when no tier has isAirportRideEnabled=true.
// Skip: there's nothing to toggle and QDI.updateAirportSwitch won't be meaningful.
console.log('[skip] canSwitchToAirport is null (no isAirportRideEnabled tier in city config)');
// Toggle the value — backend propagates it via QDI.updateAirportSwitch.
// canSwitchToAirport' = any non-restricted tier with isAirportRideEnabled

item.description:
POST /driver/updateServiceTiers with canSwitchToAirport flipped.

Backend in postDriverUpdateServiceTiers:
  canSwitchToAirport' = foldl (\acc (tier, isRestricted) ->
    if isRestricted then acc
    else acc || (fromMaybe driverInfo.canSwitchToAirport req.canSwitchToAirport
                 && fromMaybe True tier.isAirportRideEnabled)) False tiers
  QDI.updateAirportSwitch canSwitchToAirport' personId

Skips gracefully if canSwitchToAirport is null (no isAirportRideEnabled tier in config).

item.name: canSwitchToAirport reflects toggle (Driver)

exec: //   canSwitchToAirport' = any non-restricted tier has isAirportRideEnabled=True
//     if canSwitchToAirport' then Just driverInfo.canSwitchToAirport else Nothing
// So only assert if canSwitchToAirport is non-null (means airport tiers exist in city config).
pm.test('canSwitchToAirport persisted and reflected via updateServiceTiers', function(){
} else { console.log('[info] canSwitchToAirport toggle skipped (no airport tiers in city config)'); }
console.log('=== Vehicle Service Tier Flow — Complete ===');
console.log('canSwitchToAirport:', d.canSwitchToAirport, '| canSwitchToRental:', d.canSwitchToRental, '| canSwitchToInterCity:', d.canSwitchToInterCity);

item.description:
Verifies canSwitchToAirport was persisted and reflected in GET.

In getDriverVehicleServiceTiers:
  canSwitchToAirport' = any non-restricted tier has isAirportRideEnabled=True
  response.canSwitchToAirport =
    if canSwitchToAirport' then Just driverInfo.canSwitchToAirport else Nothing

So if no tier has isAirportRideEnabled, the field is always null regardless of toggle.
Assertion skipped in that case.
